info.title: Tickets
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: When a customer contacts your support team to request help with an issue, create a ticket to track that request and keep a record of it for future reference.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the tickets API to create and manage CRM records that represent customer service requests in your CRM.

post.summary: Read tickets by ID or property

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Returns a batch of tickets retrieved by internal ID or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{ticketId}`. `{ticketId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: ticketId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{ticketId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: ticketId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{ticketId}`or optionally a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `{ticketId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, and the `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will result in an error. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: ticketId
parameters.description: The unique internal ID of the ticket to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

post.summary: Merge two tickets with same type
post.description: Returns the merged ticket object resulting from combining two tickets of the same type.

post.summary: Archive a batch of tickets by ID
post.description: Archives a batch of tickets by ID. Returns no content on success (204).
Returns the created batch of ticket objects, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

post.summary: Update tickets by ID or property
post.description: Returns the updated batch of ticket objects identified by internal ID or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read a page of tickets. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a ticket with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard tickets is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Returns a list of tickets matching the specified search criteria and filters.
